name: Empanadas De Fruta
recipeIngredient: 20 ounces Mixed dried fruit (e.g., raisins, apricots)
1 cup Pinon nuts (other nuts are acceptable)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, salt, and sugar.
2. Add shortening to the dry ingredients and work it in until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups of water while stirring with a fork or pastry blender until the dough holds together without being wet or sticky.
4. Divide the dough into six equal pieces, shape each into a ball, and cover with plastic wrap.
5. Roll out each piece of dough to a circle about 5 inches in diameter and 1/8 inch thick using a floured rolling pin.
6. Place a heaping teaspoon of fruit filling on one half of the rolled-out dough; fold over the other half and press the edges together firmly to seal, giving the dough a slight half-turn.
7. Heat oil (for frying) in a deep skillet or pot to 350°F (175°C).
8. Fry the empanadas until golden brown, turning occasionally, for about 3-4 minutes per side.
9. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
10. For the fruit filling: Combine dried fruit, raisins, enough water to cover them in the pot, then bring to a simmer over low heat until tender. Stir in sugar, spices, and nuts; mix well. Alternatively, use 1 can (21 oz) of prepared fruit pie filling.
Note: To avoid overheating, turn off the burner once the oil reaches the desired temperature.
